title: History of the United States
summary: How geographic expansion, constitutional innovation, and continuous moral reckoning shaped a continental republic into a global superpower.

context.headline: A continent forged in contradiction
context.description: From colonial periphery to global hegemon, the U.S. grew through territorial acquisition, civil war, and industrial mobilization. Its national identity was continuously rebuilt through the tension between founding ideals and lived reality, transforming regional conflict into constitutional framework and wartime industry into worldwide influence.
